rawIntent:
[Feature: Add a status module: a GET /status route returning JSON {up:true,version:'1'} — Phase 1: Phase 1: Create status domain model, service interface, and service implementation]

Create src/modules/status/status.model.ts defining the SystemStatus transient value object with attributes `up: boolean` and `version: string`. Create src/modules/status/status.service.interface.ts defining the IStatusService interface with a single method `getStatus(): SystemStatus`. Create src/modules/status/status.service.ts implementing StatusService — the `getStatus()` method returns `{ up: true, version: '1' }`. No database, no repository, no external dependencies. This phase has no prior-phase dependencies.

Phase architecture notes:
SystemStatus is a transient value object computed inline and discarded after the response. IStatusService and StatusService are the canonical names from the architecture spec. No repository needed — purely read-only.

Detailed phase architecture (architecture-agent):

constraints: No database access — SystemStatus is a transient value object with no persistence
No repository pattern needed — purely read-only inline computation
No audit records required — no state-changing operations in this phase
No input validation needed — no API boundaries in this phase
No RBAC enforcement needed — no API endpoints in this phase
No sensitive data in logs — the returned values (up: true, version: '1') contain no PII, tokens, or secrets
All async errors must be caught and handled — though getStatus() is synchronous, the pattern must be followed if async wrappers are added later
Do not add @gestalt/* packages as project dependencies
Use unknown with type guards instead of any — though no any usage is expected in this simple phase
outOfScope: Phase 2 — registering GET /status endpoint on Fastify instance
Any database tables, migrations, or repository implementations
Any cross-module dependencies on leave, balance, employee, policy, notification, or audit modules
Any external npm dependencies beyond what already exists in package.json
